story: At 10:30 a.m. I took a frantic call from Douglas Feathwell, manager of a five-story building. I arrived to a bull mastiff brawling with two monkeys and a boa constrictor loose. On the fifth floor a man assaulted his partner; she hurled a heavy object that shattered a wall-length aquarium, and water and tropical fish poured through ceilings as I arrested him and called an ambulance. As the flood spread, the empty fourth floor was soaked; a corridor light there fizzed and spat tiny sparks as water seeped into its wiring. Downstairs, small fish already flopped in puddles on the landings; parrots shrieked; the first-floor tenant went to retrieve his snake. Douglas hovered in the lobby, unsure where to start.
implausible_knowledge: a corridor light there fizzed and spat tiny sparks as water seeped into its wiring
question: What will Douglas most likely do next in response to the flooding and chaos?
answer_with_knowledge: Shut off the building’s main power immediately and call the fire department, then begin evacuating residents.
answer_without_knowledge: Grab a bucket and start collecting the stranded tropical fish from the hallways.
